top_signals.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 22
top_signals.summary: Oil jumped above $95, extending this month’s price jump to 30%, as the US and Iran played down the prospect of talks and disruptions to global supplies continued to mount.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The US has collected about $13bn of Venezuela’s oil money. Where is it?
top_signals.summary: Washington has given diverging accounts of where the earthquake-shattered country’s funds are going
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: The Fed rang the alarm about Anthropic's Mythos AI model — but had to go months without it
top_signals.summary: The central bank didn't have access to Claude Mythos Preview as of mid-July, even as other institutions were racing to patch their vulnerabilities.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.summary: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former chief lending officer of Heritage State Bank

top_signals.title: Guidance: Royal Mint Trading Fund: framework document
top_signals.summary: This document sets out the accountabilities and relationships between the Royal Mint Trading Fund, the Shareholder (HM Treasury) and the Shareholder Representative (UK Government Investments).

top_signals.title: Prologis Makes £14 Billion Best and Final Offer for Segro
top_signals.summary: Prologis Inc. has submitted a best and final offer for Segro Plc that values the UK’s largest publicly traded landlord at about £14 billion ($18.7 billion).
